perturbation.original_text: Nothing contained in this Section 1 shall be deemed to prevent a Stockholder from (a) leasing or renting real property in which the Stockholder owns a direct or <PAGE> indirect interest to a person or entity engaged in the Business; or (b) beneficially owning, directly or indirectly, 5% or less of any class of securities of an entity that has a class of securities registered under Section 12 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, so long as such securities ownership does not include any operational, managerial or consulting relationship with such corporation or entity.
perturbation.changed_text: Nothing contained in this Section 1 shall be deemed to prevent a Stockholder from (a) leasing or renting real property in which the Stockholder owns a direct or <PAGE> indirect interest to a person or entity engaged in the Business; or (b) beneficially owning, directly or indirectly, 20% or less of any class of securities of an entity that has a class of securities registered under Section 12 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, so long as such securities ownership does not include any operational, managerial or consulting relationship with such corporation or entity.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the allowable beneficial ownership from '5% or less' to '20% or less' creates an in-text contradiction regarding acceptable investments. It is now unclear what level of securities ownership is considered a violation of the non-compete, since other sections of the agreement do not clarify the permissibility of investments between 5% and 20%.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of the Stockholders severally agrees that for a period (the "Restrictive Period") which shall commence on the Effective Date of the Merger and shall expire on a date which shall be the earlier to occur of: (a) such date as such Stockholder shall no longer be employed on a full-time basis with AUGI or any other subsidiary or affiliate of AUGI; or (b) three (3) years following the effectiveness of the Merger (the "Restrictive Period"), without the prior written consent of AUGI, such Stockholder shall not invest, carry on, engage or become involved, either as an employee, agent, advisor, officer, director, stockholder, manager, partner, joint venturer, participant or consultant in any business enterprise (other than Mergerco, AUGI or any of their respective subsidiaries, affiliates, successors or assigns) which derives any material revenues from the offer or sale in the United States from time to time during the Restrictive Period of services relating to all aspects of providing site acquisition, zoning, architectural and engineering consulting and design services to the wireless telecommunications industry (the "Business"), or which engages in any other business substantially similar to and directly competitive with the Business conducted by the Company as at the date hereof. Nothing contained in this Section 1 shall be deemed to prevent a Stockholder from (a) leasing or renting real property in which the Stockholder owns a direct or <PAGE> indirect interest to a person or entity engaged in the Business; or (b) beneficially owning, directly or indirectly, 20% or less of any class of securities of an entity that has a class of securities registered under Section 12 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, so long as such securities ownership does not include any operational, managerial or consulting relationship with such corporation or entity.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for beneficial ownership of 5% or less of securities. The changed text increases this to 20% or less. This creates an inconsistency within the Restrictive Period outlined in the Contradicted Text. The increased allowable ownership creates ambiguity, as the Restrictive Period aims to prevent stockholders from becoming too involved in competing businesses, and the change makes it unclear what level of involvement is considered a violation.

perturbation.original_text: The term "Confidential Information" does not include information which information and its relevance in the particular instance (A) is a matter of common knowledge or public record, (B) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by the Stockholder, (C) which information and its relevance is generally known in Business, (D) the Stockholder can demonstrate that such information and its relevance in the applicable instance, was already known to the recipient thereof other than by reason of any breach of any obligation of the Stockholder under this Agreement or any other confidentiality or non-disclosure agreement, or (E) the information is required to be disclosed by law or pursuant to court order or subpoeona.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Confidential Information" does not include information which information and its relevance in the particular instance (A) is a matter of common knowledge or public record, (B) is or becomes generally available to the public even as a result of disclosure by the Stockholder, (C) which information and its relevance is generally known in Business, (D) the Stockholder can demonstrate that such information and its relevance in the applicable instance, was already known to the recipient thereof other than by reason of any breach of any obligation of the Stockholder under this Agreement or any other confidentiality or non-disclosure agreement, or (E) the information is required to be disclosed by law or pursuant to court order or subpoeona.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'other than as a result of disclosure by the Stockholder' to 'even as a result of disclosure by the Stockholder' contradicts the fundamental purpose of a confidentiality agreement. The original text protects the company from the stockholder making information publicly available. The changed text eliminates this protection, creating uncertainty about the scope of information that is actually considered 'Confidential Information.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of Stockholders severally agrees that such Stockholder shall not, for a period of five (5) years following the effectiveness of the Merger, print, publish, divulge or communicate to any person, firm, corporation or other business organization or use for his or their own account any business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, product designs and specifications, know-how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, promotional materials or other secret or confidential information relating to the Business or any other businesses then being engaged in by AUGI or any of its subsidiaries, or any secret or confidential information relating to the affairs, dealings and concerns of the Company, AUGI or any of the subsidiaries of AUGI (collectively, the "Confidential Information") which the Stockholder has received or obtained while an employee or member of the Board of Directors of AUGI or the Company (whether or not developed, devised, or otherwise created in whole or in part by the efforts of the Stockholder). The term "Confidential Information" does not include information which information and its relevance in the particular instance (A) is a matter of common knowledge or public record, (B) is or becomes generally available to the public even as a result of disclosure by the Stockholder, (C) which information and its relevance is generally known in Business, (D) the Stockholder can demonstrate that such information and its relevance in the applicable instance, was already known to the recipient thereof other than by reason of any breach of any obligation of the Stockholder under this Agreement or any other confidentiality or non-disclosure agreement, or (E) the information is required to be disclosed by law or pursuant to court order or subpoeona.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information' to include information that becomes publicly available even as a result of disclosure by the stockholder. The contradicted text states that the stockholder shall not disclose Confidential Information. By changing the definition of Confidential Information, the agreement now allows the stockholder to disclose information that would have originally been protected. The changed text contradicts the intent of the agreement. This is a strong contradiction because the core obligation of confidentiality is undermined.

perturbation.original_text: Without intending to limit the remedies available to AUGI or the Company, each Stockholder further agrees that damages at law will be an insufficient remedy to AUGI if such Stockholder(s) breaches the terms of this Agreement and that AUGI or the Company may apply for and have injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction to restrain the breach or threatened breach of or otherwise to specifically enforce any of the covenants contained herein, in each case without the necessity of proving damages or posting bond.
perturbation.changed_text: Without intending to limit the remedies available to AUGI or the Company, each Stockholder further agrees that damages at law will be a sufficient remedy to AUGI if such Stockholder(s) breaches the terms of this Agreement and that AUGI or the Company may only pursue monetary damages in any court of competent jurisdiction for a breach or threatened breach of the covenants contained herein.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'damages at law will be an insufficient remedy' to 'damages at law will be a sufficient remedy' and restricting AUGI's remedies to 'only monetary damages' directly contradicts the original intent to allow for injunctive relief. This in-text contradiction limits the legal recourse available to AUGI in case of a breach, making enforcement weaker and creating uncertainty about available remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without intending to limit the remedies available to AUGI or the Company, each Stockholder further agrees that damages at law will be a sufficient remedy to AUGI if such Stockholder(s) breaches the terms of this Agreement and that AUGI or the Company may only pursue monetary damages in any court of competent jurisdiction for a breach or threatened breach of the covenants contained herein, in each case without the necessity of proving damages or posting bond.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that 'damages at law will be a sufficient remedy' and limits AUGI to 'only monetary damages'. This directly contradicts the original text's allowance for injunctive relief and its assertion that damages at law would be an 'insufficient remedy'. The contradiction is present even if the texts are the same, because the changes introduce the contradiction into the text.
